message:
feat(writing/wills-estate): flesh out hero, body callouts, and viz system; wire visualizations through error boundary

Major content + visualization pass on the wills & estate planning
article. Fills the hgjp.7 subtitle / stat-chip / scroll-indicator stubs
and completes the prose + visual flow the article was drafted around.

components/wills-estate-article.tsx
- Replace the manual `next/dynamic` import block for each chart with a
  single import from `components/viz-error-boundary`. That module already
  wraps the dynamic(ssr:false) imports in an error boundary, so a
  runtime failure in any one chart renders a friendly fallback instead
  of taking down the whole article.
- Remove the now-dead `dynamic` import and the `void <symbol>`
  unused-variable-suppression block that was parked for unfinished
  stubs.
- Drop the "TODO: body from hgjp.7" subtitle placeholder and ship the
  real subtitle prose.
- Add a hero stat-chip row (purple / cyan / emerald) summarising the
  201 files · 17 subagents · 45 templates payload, the Tier 1-5 reach,
  and the price-point agnosticism — matches the editorial pattern
  established by other article heroes on this site.
- Add the hero scroll indicator element.
- Substantial body-copy expansion throughout sections downstream of the
  hero (too large to enumerate line by line; see file diff).

components/wills-estate-visualizations.tsx
- Retire the local `VizShell` + `useViewportFlags` helpers and fold the
  mobile / reduced-motion / notes-toggle behaviour into the individual
  chart components that needed it. This removes a layer of indirection
  that was making it hard to theme individual visualizations.
- Broaden the lucide icon vocabulary (AlertCircle, Baby, Eye, Feather,
  FileText, FileWarning, GitFork, Globe, Inbox, Lock, Shield,
  ShieldAlert) so the new tier-specific visuals have the affordances
  they need without custom SVG.
- Replace `useInView` driven animation with `useReducedMotion` +
  self-managed triggers where the in-view behaviour was misfiring on
  rapid scroll.
- Flesh out TierTriageViz, AxiomCoherenceViz, IntakePhasesViz,
  DeliverablesTreeViz, and AntiPatternCardsViz with production-quality
  illustrations (see file diff for the per-chart updates).

Skipped `.beads/.write.lock` (ephemeral sqlite advisory lock, already
gitignored).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

    • +

    • +

+ Is this legal advice? +
+

+ No. The skill produces audit documents, drafts, and a handoff + packet for your attorney. Your attorney is the one who actually + advises you, drafts the final documents, and signs off on + execution. Nothing the skill produces creates an + attorney-client relationship with anyone. +

+
+
+ +
+ Do I still need an attorney? +
+

+ Yes. For anyone with more than a trivial estate, minor + children, a blended family, a business, disability in the + family, or unusual circumstances, you want a real attorney. + The skill makes that attorney's first meeting take two + hours instead of ten. It does not replace the meeting. +

+
+
+ +
+ Is my data private? +
+

+ Yes. The agent runs on your computer, reading files you + explicitly give it. It does not upload your financial + documents, your tax returns, or your family history to any + server. Nothing leaves your laptop except — at your + direction — the sanitized Attorney Engagement Brief you + choose to send your lawyer. +

+
+
+ +
+ What if I'm not in the United States? +
+

+ The skill is U.S.-only. It covers federal law plus all fifty + states. If you are a U.S. citizen domiciled abroad with U.S. + assets, it handles the cross-border overlay. If you are not + U.S.-domiciled, this is not the right tool for you yet. +

+ Will the skill work after tax law changes? +
+

+ The skill separates evergreen methodology from volatile law. + Every federal threshold, state exemption, and execution + formality is verified against primary sources at the time of + use and logged in official-source-log.md. When + the 2026 OBBBA rules change, the skill + picks up the new numbers. Old plans carry their own + verification trail so you can see exactly what needs + re-checking. +

+
+

+ Why a subscription instead of a one-time purchase? +

+ Because the thing you are paying for — the living, verified + set of axioms plus the reference corpus plus the subagents + plus the updates when the law changes — is not a one-time + thing. A static PDF of estate-planning templates from three + years ago is a trap, not a service. Cancel anytime. The + artifacts you have produced stay on your computer after you + cancel. +
